note: Frozen SALSA baseline. Rebuild with: node scripts/build-salsa-baseline.mjs

checklists.name: Closing Checks
checklists.description: PM shift closing checks — complete at end of production.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: All records completed and signed off
questions.hint: All batch records, cleaning logs and checklists filled in for today

questions.label: Stock rotation completed
questions.hint: First In, First Out — oldest stock to the front

questions.label: All knives and sharps present and intact
Bin bags changed and bins cleaned
Has raw material reconciliation been completed?
questions.hint: Check quantity remaining

questions.label: Fridge temperature (°C)
questions.hint: Must be 1–8°C. If outside range, do not store product and contact manager.

questions.label: Equipment faults or maintenance issues
questions.hint: Leave blank if none — raise a Maintenance Report if needed

questions.label: Notes and handover
questions.hint: Anything the next shift or manager needs to know

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Complaint Form
checklists.description: Customer or internal complaint record — one form per complaint.

questions.label: Recorded by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Complainant contact details
questions.hint: Phone and/or email

questions.label: Product name and batch number
questions.hint: Include Julian code / lot number if known

questions.label: Root cause identified
questions.hint: Investigate and document — a corrective action may be required

questions.label: Corrective action raised
questions.hint: Tick if a corrective action report has been raised

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager name

checklists.name: Corrective Action Report
checklists.description: Non-conformance and corrective action record.

questions.label: Reference number (e.g. CAR-001)
questions.hint: Use sequential numbering e.g. CAR-001

questions.label: Description of non-conformance
questions.hint: What happened? Include product, process, date and location.

questions.label: Products / batches affected
questions.hint: Include batch/lot numbers and quantities

questions.label: Immediate containment action
questions.hint: What was done straight away to prevent further risk?

questions.label: Root cause analysis
questions.hint: Why did this happen? Use 5-Whys or similar approach.

questions.label: Effectiveness review date
questions.hint: Date to verify the corrective action has been effective

questions.label: Closed out
questions.hint: Tick when corrective action is verified as effective

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager name

checklists.name: Daily Cleaning
checklists.description: End-of-day cleaning record — complete after each production day.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Worktops cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Jantex MSSC · 1/10 dilution · 30s contact · wipe dry with blue paper towel

questions.label: Scales cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Jantex MSSC · 1/10 dilution · 30s contact · wipe dry with blue paper towel

questions.label: Equipment sinks cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Wash with Jantex Washing Up Liquid and hot water · rinse · spray Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact · wipe dry

questions.label: Dishwasher and baskets cleaned and sanitised
Hand contact points cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Door handles, light switches, push plates · Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact

questions.label: Bins cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Remove debris · spray Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact · wipe dry with blue paper towel

questions.label: Production floor swept and mopped
questions.hint: Sweep first to remove debris · mop with Jantex Floor Maintainer at 1/20 in hot water · allow to air dry

questions.label: Any areas not completed — state reason
questions.hint: Leave blank if everything was done. Write N/A if an item is not in use today.

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Employee Induction Checklist
checklists.description: Complete for every new employee on their first day.

questions.label: Induction completed by
questions.hint: Trainer or manager name

questions.label: Completed Food Safety booklet questionnaire
questions.hint: Must achieve >80% to pass, can reattempt if required

questions.label: Visitor Sign In Log — explained and understood
Personal Hygiene Rules — explained and understood
questions.hint: Including handwashing, PPE, jewellery policy, illness reporting

questions.label: PPE requirements — explained and issued
questions.hint: Hairnet, apron, gloves — correct donning and doffing demonstrated

questions.label: Food Safety Booklet — received and read
Level 2 Food Safety & Hygiene Training completed?
HACCP and food safety basics — explained
questions.hint: SALSA standard, HACCP principles, CCPs, temperature control

questions.label: Cleaning procedures — explained
questions.hint: Daily, weekly and monthly schedules. Chemicals and correct dilutions.

questions.label: Return to Work procedure — explained
questions.hint: 48-hour rule, self-assessment form, manager approval required

questions.label: Premises orientation and safety briefing — completed
questions.hint: Site tour, storage areas, welfare facilities, restricted areas

questions.label: Fire safety — explained
questions.hint: Fire exits, assembly point, extinguisher locations, evacuation procedure

questions.label: I confirm the above has been received and fully understood

checklists.name: Food Safety Booklet Questionnaire
checklists.description: Knowledge check to confirm new starters understand food safety basics.

questions.label: Q1. What food safety standard does Yep Kitchen work towards?
Q2. What does HACCP stand for?
Q3. What is the bacterial danger zone?
questions.hint: The temperature range where bacteria multiply rapidly

questions.label: Q4. How long must you be free from diarrhoea or vomiting before returning to food handling duties?
Q5. Where must you wash your hands?
Q6. Which PPE must you wear in the production area?
options: Hairnet or cap, protective coat and gloves

questions.label: Q7. What should you do if there is a glass or brittle plastic breakage?
options: Stop work, isolate the area, inform your manager immediately
Clear it up yourself and carry on
Ignore it and finish the task
Wait until end of shift to report it

questions.label: Q8. What does cross-contamination mean?
options: The transfer of harmful substances or allergens from one surface, food or person to another

questions.label: Q9. What is the definition of malicious contamination?
options: Deliberate contamination of a product or raw material with intent to cause harm

questions.label: Q10. How can you reduce the risk of pest infestation?
options: Clean spills immediately, keep waste in bins, report any damage to building fabric
Move pest control bait boxes to a better location
Ignore signs of pest activity unless very obvious

questions.label: Score (out of 10)
questions.hint: For manager use — total number of correct answers

questions.label: Result
questions.hint: Pass = 8 or more correct (80%). Fail = further training required before commencing duties.

questions.label: Assessed by
questions.hint: Manager or trainer name

checklists.name: Goods In Record
checklists.description: Record every delivery received — one form per delivery.

questions.label: Please upload a photo of the delivery note.
Delivery vehicle in good condition and free from PCM contamination?
Reason if rejected or partially accepted

checklists.name: Goods Out Record
checklists.description: Record every dispatch — one form per dispatch.

questions.label: Please upload a photo of the delivery note.
Collection vehicle in good condition and free from PCM contamination?

checklists.name: Hygiene Swab
checklists.description: Environmental hygiene swab results record.

questions.label: Swab kit / brand used
questions.hint: e.g. Hygiena SystemSURE Plus

questions.label: Surface or area swabbed — 1
questions.hint: e.g. Cooking kettle inner surface

questions.label: Any fails — corrective action taken
questions.hint: Describe action taken and re-swab date

checklists.name: Laundry Checklist
checklists.description: Weekly laundry and PPE condition record.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Number of items washed
questions.hint: Aprons, cloths, towels etc.

questions.label: Temperature washed at (°C)
questions.hint: Minimum 60°C for effective sanitisation

questions.label: Lint and fluff filters cleaned
questions.hint: Clean before or after each wash cycle

questions.label: All PPE in good condition
questions.hint: Check for wear, tears or damage — replace any items as needed

questions.label: Laundry indicator strip used (quarterly only)
questions.hint: Required once per quarter to verify wash temperature is being reached

questions.label: Indicator strip confirmed temperature >60°C (quarterly only)
questions.hint: Fail: do not use laundry — contact manager

questions.label: Water filter and soap dispenser cleaned (quarterly only)
questions.hint: Required once per quarter

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Maintenance Report
checklists.description: Log any equipment faults, building issues, or repairs needed.

questions.label: Reported by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Equipment or area
questions.hint: e.g. Cooking kettle, hot filling line, production floor drain

questions.label: Maintenance start time
questions.hint: 24hr format e.g. 08:30

questions.label: Maintenance end time
questions.hint: 24hr format

questions.label: Temporary repair details
questions.hint: If a permanent fix is still required, describe the interim measure used

questions.label: Parts required
questions.hint: Description, quantity and part number if known

questions.label: Food safety risk?
options: Yes — equipment taken out of service
questions.hint: If Yes — take equipment out of service immediately

questions.label: Equipment taken out of service during maintenance
Contractor name
questions.hint: Leave blank if in-house repair

questions.label: HYGIENE CLEARANCE: All machine parts accounted for
questions.hint: Confirm no parts left inside or near the equipment

questions.label: HYGIENE CLEARANCE: Equipment cleared of all maintenance debris
questions.hint: Tools, swarf, packaging, rags etc. all removed

questions.label: HYGIENE CLEARANCE: Equipment cleaned and sanitised prior to use
Equipment accepted back into production use
Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Production manager name

checklists.name: Monthly Cleaning
checklists.description: Monthly deep clean and maintenance check.

questions.label: Month / year
questions.hint: e.g. May 2026

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Inaccessible floor areas cleaned
questions.hint: Behind and under large equipment, corners, drains

questions.label: Shelves cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact · rinse · dry with blue paper towel

questions.label: Walls cleaned
questions.hint: Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact · dry with blue paper towel or air dry

questions.label: Plug sockets cleaned
questions.hint: Ensure power is isolated before cleaning

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Opening Checks
checklists.description: AM shift opening checks — complete before production begins.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Handwash basins stocked with soap and paper towels
No signs of pest activity
questions.hint: Check bait stations, look for droppings or gnaw marks

questions.label: Fridge temperature (°C)
questions.hint: Must be 1–8°C. If outside range, do not use and contact manager immediately.

questions.label: Glass and brittle plastic register checked — all items present and intact
Key equipment in working order
questions.hint: Any faults — raise a Maintenance Report

questions.label: All knives and sharps present and intact
All staff fit for work and PPE worn correctly
questions.hint: No illness, cuts, or infections · hair covered · apron and gloves on

questions.label: Any issues to report
questions.hint: Leave blank if none

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Pre-employment medical questionnaire
checklists.description: Checklist must be completed on a new employees first day

questions.label: Are you currently suffering from any of the following symptoms?
Do you have or have you suffered from Typhoid / Paratyphoid Fever?
Do you have or have you suffered from Dysentery?
Do you have or have you suffered from Salmonella?
Do you have or have you suffered from running ear or perforated ear?
Do you have or have you suffered from eye infections?
Do you have or have you suffered from recurring cold sores?
Do you have or have you suffered from a frequent sore throat?
Do you have or have you suffered from persistent colds / flu Like symptoms?
Do you have or have you suffered from cczema or other skin disorders?
Have you suffered from diarrhoea or vomiting in the last 7 days?
Has anyone in your household suffered from diarrhoea or vomiting in the last 7 days?
Have you returned from overseas in the past 3 months?
Have you been in contact with anyone who has Typhoid, Parathyphoid, Enteric fever, or Cholera?
Do you need to have any medicines with you on site?
questions.hint: For example, inhalers for asthma

questions.label: Do you wear contact lenses or glasses?
Do you suffer from any food allergies?
Please sign to confirm the information you have provided is accurate.

checklists.name: Probe Calibration
checklists.description: Temperature probe calibration check.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Reference probe ID
questions.hint: The calibrated reference probe used for comparison

questions.label: Production probe ID
questions.hint: The probe being checked

questions.label: Iced water — reference probe reading (°C)
questions.hint: Should read approximately 0°C

questions.label: Iced water — production probe within ±1°C of reference
questions.hint: Fail: withdraw probe, label "Do Not Use", inform manager

questions.label: Freshly boiled water — reference probe reading (°C)
questions.hint: Should read approximately 100°C

questions.label: Freshly boiled water — production probe reading (°C)
Freshly boiled water — production probe within ±1°C of reference
questions.hint: Fail: withdraw probe, label "Do Not Use", inform manager

questions.label: Comments or corrective action
questions.hint: Leave blank if both checks passed

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Return to Work Approval
checklists.description: Manager approval before a staff member returns after illness.

questions.label: Did employee have diarrhoea or vomiting during absence?
If yes — employee must be symptom-free for 48 hours before return — confirmed?
Cleared to return to full duties?
options: Yes — light duties only (no direct food contact)

questions.label: Medical advice sought?
questions.hint: Required if symptoms suggest a notifiable illness

questions.label: Total sickness absences this year (including this one)
Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Return to Work Self Assessment
checklists.description: Employee self-assessment to complete before returning after illness.

questions.label: Nature of illness or reason for absence
questions.hint: Be specific — e.g. "vomiting and diarrhoea" not just "illness"

questions.label: Have you travelled abroad in the last 2 weeks?
questions.hint: If Yes and you were ill during the trip, you must be symptom-free for 48 hours before returning to food handling duties

questions.label: I have been symptom-free for at least 48 hours
I do not have any open cuts or sores on my hands (or they are fully covered with a blue plaster)
I confirm the above statements are true
Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Scale Calibration
checklists.description: Weighing scale accuracy check.

questions.label: Calibration weight used (g)
questions.hint: Use the certified calibration weight for this scale

questions.label: Scale reading (g)
questions.hint: Check 5 positions: TLH, TRH, BLH, BRH, Centre — record the highest reading

questions.label: Within ±1g tolerance?
questions.hint: Pass: deviation ≤1g. Fail: take scale out of use and contact manager.

questions.label: Scale 2 calibration weight (g)
questions.hint: Use the certified calibration weight for this scale

questions.label: Scale 2 reading (g)
questions.hint: Check 5 positions: TLH, TRH, BLH, BRH, Centre — record the highest reading

questions.label: Scale 2 within ±1g tolerance?
questions.hint: Pass: deviation ≤1g. Fail: take scale out of use and contact manager.

questions.label: Out of tolerance — corrective action taken
questions.hint: Leave blank if all scales passed

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

checklists.name: Visitor Sign In & Health Questionnaire
checklists.description: All visitors to the production area must complete this form.

questions.label: Time in
questions.hint: 24hr format e.g. 09:30

questions.label: I have read and understood the personal hygiene rules.
Do you currently have a high temperature, persistent cough, or loss of taste or smell?
questions.hint: If Yes, you are not permitted on site

questions.label: In the past 7 days have you had food poisoning, diarrhoea or vomiting?
Do you have any skin infections, boils, or discharge from eyes, ears or nose?
Have you travelled abroad in the last 2 weeks?
If you answered Yes to any health question — are you symptom-free for at least 48 hours?
options: N/A — answered No to all health questions
questions.hint: If No, you are not permitted on site

questions.label: Have you read and understood the site hygiene and visitor rules?
Are you aware we handle allergens on site — do you have any relevant allergies?
questions.hint: If Yes, inform your host before entering production areas

questions.label: Visitor briefed on emergency procedures
questions.hint: Fire exits, assembly point, nearest first aider

checklists.name: Weekly Cleaning
checklists.description: Weekly deep clean record.

questions.label: Completed by
questions.hint: Your name

questions.label: Changing area cleaned
questions.hint: Including lockers and coat hooks

questions.label: Toilets cleaned
questions.hint: Toilet cleaner as per pack instructions · sweep floor · red mop bucket only · Jantex Floor Maintainer at 1/20

questions.label: Chiller, fridge and blast chiller cleaned and sanitised
questions.hint: Move stock to one side · remove debris · spray Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · 30s contact · rinse · wipe dry

questions.label: Warehouse and storage area cleaned
questions.hint: Surfaces: Jantex MSSC at 1/10 · Floors: Jantex Floor Maintainer at 1/20

questions.label: Checked and signed off by
questions.hint: Manager or supervisor name

saq_questions.question_text: Company / trading name
Full address (including postcode)
Main contact name
Job title / position
Email address
Telephone number
Do you hold a recognised food safety certification? (e.g. BRCGS, IFS, FSSC 22000, SQF, SALSA, ISO 22000)
Name of certification(s) held
Certifying body
Certificate number
Certificate expiry date
Are your audits unannounced?
Do you have a documented food safety policy signed by senior management?
Do you have a documented HACCP plan in place?
Are all Critical Control Points (CCPs) monitored and records maintained?
Is your HACCP plan reviewed at least annually or after any significant change?
Is your HACCP system verified by a competent person?
Are corrective actions documented and followed up?
Do you conduct internal audits of your food safety systems?
Do you have a documented product recall / withdrawal procedure?
Do you have a documented customer complaints procedure?
Do you have a documented supplier approval and monitoring procedure?
Is the site and building maintained in a good state of repair?
Is there a documented and active pest control programme in place, with visits recorded and reviewed?
Is there a documented waste management procedure?
Is the water used in production potable and tested regularly?
Is there a documented preventive maintenance programme for food contact equipment?
Is measuring and monitoring equipment calibrated at defined intervals, with records maintained?
Do you have a documented personal hygiene policy for all food handlers?
Is there a procedure for reporting illness and exclusion of food handlers?
Are hand-washing facilities available at all critical points in production?
Are visitor hygiene requirements documented and enforced?
Do you have a documented cleaning schedule for all food contact surfaces and equipment?
Are cleaning records maintained and the effectiveness of cleaning verified (e.g. swabbing, visual inspection)?
Are cleaning chemicals approved for use in a food environment and stored separately from food?
Do you have a documented allergen management policy with a completed allergen risk assessment for all products?
Are allergenic ingredients physically segregated during storage and production?
Do you have allergen-specific cleaning procedures to prevent cross-contact?
Are allergens correctly declared on product labels and/or specifications?
Are all relevant staff trained in allergen awareness?
Do you have a documented foreign body detection and control procedure?
Do you have a glass and brittle plastic policy?
Is metal detection or X-ray equipment used where appropriate?
Is colour-coded equipment used to prevent cross-contamination?
Do you have up-to-date written product specifications for all products supplied to us?
Do your products comply with applicable food labelling legislation?
Are shelf-life / durability studies carried out and documented?
Do you conduct microbiological and/or chemical product testing?
If yes, how frequently is testing carried out?
Are all products batch-coded to allow full traceability?
Do you have documented process controls (e.g. temperature, time, pH) with monitoring records maintained?
Can you trace all raw materials from receipt through to finished product despatch?
Do you conduct traceability exercises to verify your system?
Are raw materials and finished goods stored under appropriate conditions (temperature, humidity)?
Are storage temperatures monitored and recorded?
Is a FIFO (first in, first out) system applied to stock rotation?
Are incoming deliveries inspected and documented?
Are delivery vehicles maintained in a clean and hygienic condition?
Have all food handlers received appropriate food hygiene training?
Has management received advanced food safety / HACCP training?
Are documented training records maintained for all food handlers?
Is training refreshed at regular intervals or when practices change?
Do any of the products you supply contain or are derived from genetically modified organisms (GMOs)?
If yes, please provide details
saq_questions.placeholder: Product name(s) and GMO ingredient details

saq_questions.question_text: Have any of the products you supply been treated with ionising radiation?
If yes, please provide details
Any additional information you would like to share
Full name of person completing this questionnaire
Job title / position
Date
